document.title: Building AI Agents with RAG
document.description: A practical guide to retrieval-augmented generation for enterprise knowledge bases and task-oriented agents.

body.raw:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become the standard pattern for grounding large language models in private, up-to-date knowledge. In this article, I walk through a production-ready architecture.

## Why RAG?

Pure LLMs hallucinate on domain-specific facts. RAG injects relevant context at inference time, improving accuracy while keeping models smaller and cheaper.

## Architecture Overview

1. **Ingestion** — Parse PDFs, docs, and wikis into chunks with metadata.
2. **Embedding** — Store vectors in PostgreSQL with `pgvector` or a dedicated vector DB.
3. **Retrieval** — Hybrid search combining dense embeddings and BM25 sparse retrieval.
4. **Generation** — Prompt the LLM with retrieved chunks and strict citation rules.

```python
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from langchain.vectorstores import PGVector

vectorstore = PGVector(connection_string=DATABASE_URL, embedding_function=embeddings)
qa = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(llm=llm, retriever=vectorstore.as_retriever())
```

## Key Lessons

- Chunk size matters: 512–1024 tokens with overlap works well for technical docs.
- Always log retrieval scores for debugging bad answers.
- Use rerankers when precision is critical.

## Next Steps

Combine RAG with tool-calling agents for workflows that need both knowledge lookup and API actions.

document.title: Designing Autonomous Robots
document.description: From sensors to Nav2 — how to architect mobile robots that navigate reliably in real environments.

body.raw:
Autonomous mobile robots require tight integration between perception, localization, planning, and control. Here's how I approach system design.

## The Perception Stack

Cameras and LiDAR provide complementary data. For indoor AMRs, 2D LiDAR plus depth cameras often suffice. Fuse inputs with a consistent timestamp using `message_filters` in ROS 2.

## Localization & Mapping

Start with **SLAM Toolbox** or **Cartographer** for mapping, then switch to **AMCL** or **localization-only** modes for deployment.

## Navigation with Nav2

Nav2's behavior trees let you compose recovery behaviors:

- Rotate in place when stuck
- Back up and replan on failure
- Dynamic obstacle handling with costmap updates

## Testing Strategy

Simulate in Gazebo before hardware tests. Record rosbags from production runs to replay edge cases offline.

## Results

Proper tuning reduced navigation failures by 40% in cluttered warehouse aisles — mostly from costmap inflation and controller gain adjustments.

document.title: GPT vs BERT Architecture Comparison
document.description: Understanding encoder-only, decoder-only, and encoder-decoder transformers for different ML tasks.

body.raw:
Choosing the right transformer architecture depends on your task. Here's a concise comparison.

## BERT (Encoder-Only)

- **Training**: Masked language modeling — predict hidden tokens.
- **Best for**: Classification, NER, embeddings, retrieval encoders.
- **Inference**: Bidirectional context in one pass.

## GPT (Decoder-Only)

- **Training**: Causal language modeling — predict next token.
- **Best for**: Text generation, chat, code completion, agents.
- **Inference**: Autoregressive, left-to-right.

## T5 / BART (Encoder-Decoder)

- **Best for**: Summarization, translation, seq2seq tasks.

## Practical Guidance

| Task | Recommended |
|------|-------------|
| Semantic search encoder | BERT / E5 |
| Chat / agents | GPT-class |
| Summarization | T5 / fine-tuned GPT |

Modern LLMs (GPT-4, Claude) are decoder-only at scale, while RAG systems often pair a BERT-style encoder for retrieval with a GPT decoder for generation.

document.title: ROS for Robotics Development
document.description: Getting started with ROS 2 — nodes, topics, services, and best practices for scalable robot software.

body.raw:
The Robot Operating System (ROS) remains the de facto middleware for robotics R&D and production deployments.

## ROS 1 vs ROS 2

ROS 2 brings:

- DDS-based communication (real-time friendly)
- Better security and multi-robot support
- Native Windows support
- Lifecycle nodes for managed states

## Core Concepts

**Nodes** are processes. **Topics** are async pub/sub channels. **Services** are sync RPC. **Actions** handle long-running tasks with feedback.

```bash
ros2 run turtlesim turtlesim_node
ros2 topic pub /turtle1/cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/Twist "{linear: {x: 2.0}}"
```

## Project Structure

Use colcon workspaces with clear package boundaries:

- `perception` — drivers and detectors
- `localization` — SLAM, AMCL
- `navigation` — Nav2 configs
- `bringup` — launch files

## Tips

- Always use `ros2 bag record` during field tests.
- Parameterize everything — avoid hardcoded IPs and frame IDs.
- Document your TF tree obsessively.

ROS 2 Humble on Ubuntu 22.04 is my recommended LTS stack for new projects.
